D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Heat vs Light Aging — Two Activation Energies

photo-fade follows photon flux × cross-section; thermal fade follows Arrhenius e^(−Ea/kT) — the same pigment ages very differently in a hot dark cellar vs a cool lit room

A pigment's lifetime is set by whichever process is faster: photo-degradation (rate ∝ UV flux × quantum yield) or thermal degradation (rate ∝ e^(−Ea/kT)). Above ~40 °C even in the dark, thermal breakdown wins for organic pigments; under bright UV at room temperature, photo always wins.

Drag the temperature and UV intensity. The combined rate predicts a half-life; the two contributions are shown as separate bars. Crossover regimes highlight when each mechanism dominates.
